explanation: High humidity (88%) with warm air (31.2°C) increases fungal disease risk during flowering. pH 7.5 is above the typical tomato hydroponic range (5.5–6.5), which can reduce iron uptake and cause yellowing in young leaves. EC 3.4 mS/cm is moderately high — verify drain EC before changing feed.
likely_risks: Botrytis / powdery mildew risk from high humidity
Iron lockout / micronutrient stress from elevated pH
Salt accumulation if drain EC is also high
safe_checks: Measure drain EC and compare to feed EC
Check young leaf color and veins
safety_note: Advisory only — not for direct pesticide, fertilizer dosing, or actuator control.
